latest.title: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
18 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7356I
Luceco PLC
18 June 2026
18 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
APPOINTMENT OF NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR
The Board of Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group"), a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems, is pleased to announce the appointment of Andrew Mines as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective from 1 August 2026.
Andrew is an experienced FTSE 100 executive who is currently Managing Director of the Electric Thermal Solutions division of Spirax Group plc, the global provider of thermal energy and fluid technology solutions that is playing a critical role in enabling the transition to net zero. He joined Spirax in 2019, initially as Managing Director of Watson-Marlow Fluid Technology Solutions business.
Andrew brings broad international industrial experience, having previously spent 23 years at Illinois Tool Works Inc (ITW), the S&P 500 global multi-industrial manufacturer. He was latterly Executive Vice President, Global Construction Products, where he was also a member of the ITW's executive leadership team.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"We are delighted to welcome Andrew to the Board. He brings excellent international industry experience with notable expertise in manufacturing of net zero enabling products and solutions. This aligns strongly with the structural growth opportunities from electrification that Luceco's competitive advantages position us perfectly to capture."
The Company makes this announcement in accordance with UKLR 6.4.6. There are no other matters to be disclosed under UKLR 6.4.8.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer

Note to Editors
Luceco plc (LSE:LUCE) is a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems. The Group designs and manufactures its market-leading range of wiring accessories, EV chargers, LED lighting, and portable power products at its state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, distributing them through professional, wholesale and retail channels.
For more information, please visit
www.lucecoplc.com
.

latest.title: CEO Retirement
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2774I
Luceco PLC
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
CEO RETIREMENT
Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group") announces that, following 21 years of leading the business, John Hornby has decided to retire from the Group, with effect from 31 December 2026.
John will be taking a sabbatical until late August 2026, and will remain as Chief Executive Officer and as a Board Director until 31 December, following which he will be available to the Group in an advisory capacity. During John's sabbatical, the Group's Senior Leadership Team, supported by the Board, will discharge the functions and responsibilities of the CEO.
The Board has commenced the process of identifying a permanent successor and will make a further announcement in due course.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank John for his longstanding contribution to Luceco. He has been an important part of Luceco's development, expanding the Group significantly into the successful business it is today, with a proven platform for growth. The Board and Luceco colleagues wish John the very best for the future."
John Hornby, commented:
"It has been a privilege to serve as Chief Executive Officer of Luceco and I am enormously proud of the Group's development over this period. With a strong platform for growth, underpinned by Luceco's competitive advantages and structural opportunities, the Group is confident in the prospects for the current financial year and beyond."
The trading momentum described in the Group's Q1 Trading Update published on 19 May 2026 has been sustained through Q2-to-date. The outlook for the year ending December 2026 remains consistent with the commentary provided in that announcement and accordingly, the Board continues to expect Adjusted Operating Profit to exceed £40m, with the potential for further significant outperformance dependent on Demand Flexibility. A half year trading update will be announced towards the end of July 2026.
Details of John Hornby's remuneration arrangements in connection with his retirement will be set out in the Section 430(2B) disclosure that will be made available on Luceco's website in due course, and will also be set out in the Remuneration Report.
Luceco plc

Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer

company_30s.what_they_do: Luceco designs, manufactures and sells energy‑efficient LED lighting solutions for commercial, industrial and residential markets. Their product line includes smart bulbs, panel lights and integrated lighting control systems that connect to IoT platforms.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global push for lower energy consumption and smarter building management makes efficient lighting a growing market. Luceco’s focus on modular, network‑connected products gives it a competitive edge in the emerging smart‑home and smart‑city segments.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a restructuring phase: the CEO has stepped down, new directors have joined, and the company has recently purchased its own shares to support the balance sheet. Cash burn remains a concern, but the leadership changes and share buy‑back signal an intent to stabilise operations and prepare for a future earnings run.

rns_narrative: Leadership overhaul – the CEO’s retirement and the appointment of a non‑executive director suggest a fresh strategic direction.
Capital confidence – a market purchase of shares for earnings‑before‑tax purposes indicates that insiders believe the business is worth the current valuation.
Financial transparency – recent AGM results and Q1 trading updates provide a baseline for assessing whether the company is moving toward profitability.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some community members point out that directors are buying shares, implying they think the stock is undervalued and that a turnaround is on the horizon.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that a CEO exit could stall momentum and that the company’s cash burn might outpace any upside from new leadership.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from indifference to cautious optimism as new directors step in and share purchases surface.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest, meaning the market is largely unaware of these changes and a catalyst could generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Announcement of a new product line or partnership that expands Luceco’s smart‑lighting portfolio.
Positive Q1 earnings that beat consensus and lift investor confidence.
A significant increase in trading volume that breaks the 30‑day moving‑average, indicating renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds three times the average daily trade, a price break above the last swing high, and any press release that confirms a funding round or a strategic alliance.
expect_next.timing_regime: The current APEX score suggests the stock is still in the early stages of a recovery cycle. A clear sign of mean reversion will be a sustained move toward the 50‑day moving average without a sharp pullback.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company has experienced a deep crash, and the recent insider buying and share buy‑back indicate that smart money believes the price is far below intrinsic value. If the stock rebounds to the midpoint of its pre‑crash range, upside potential is roughly four‑to‑five times the entry price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Leadership turnover could delay a turnaround, and the company’s ongoing cash burn poses a risk of further dilution if new capital is required. A worst‑case scenario would see the stock stall at its current low, offering limited upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the entry price (plus a modest stop), while upside could be 4×–5× the entry if a mean‑reversion plays out.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip below the 20‑day moving average with a position size of 10% of discretionary capital. Place a stop 10% below the entry to limit downside. Target the midpoint of the pre‑crash range and consider taking partial profits at 30% of that target.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until volume exceeds three times the 30‑day average and the price breaks above the last swing high. A positive earnings report would also be a strong trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk exists, so the default stance remains bullish pending a catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Luceco’s deep crash, combined with insider confidence and a quiet market, creates a high‑asymmetry play that could deliver a 4‑to‑5× return if a catalyst pushes the price back toward its pre‑crash midpoint.
